qoq_changes: Inventories increased to $65.35 billion from $56.44 billion at the end of the prior fiscal year, reflecting seasonal holiday build.
Short-term borrowings increased to $8.40 billion from $3.07 billion at the end of the prior fiscal year.
Cash and cash equivalents increased to $10.58 billion from $9.04 billion at the end of the prior fiscal year.
new_risks: Specific new risks were not detailed in the provided financial statement excerpts, as Item 1A (Risk Factors) was omitted from the text.
management_tone: Bullish; the company demonstrated robust 5.8% revenue growth and a significant 34.2% expansion in net income, signaling strong consumer demand and operational efficiency.
analyst_note: Walmart's Q3 2025 results show impressive bottom-line expansion and steady top-line growth. The $8.9 billion increase in inventory since January suggests management is positioning aggressively for the Q4 holiday season.
